meta.title: Kodo Forge — Free Terminal Learning Platform for TypeScript, React & Angular
meta.description: Kodo Forge is a free, open-source terminal learning platform (TUI) for mastering TypeScript, Angular, React, and Next.js. 144+ lessons, spaced repetition, kinetic reading, and gamified progress tracking — 100% offline, zero dependencies.
meta.ogTitle: Kodo Forge — Free Terminal Learning Platform
meta.ogDescription: Master TypeScript, React, Angular & Next.js from your terminal. 144+ lessons, zero dependencies, 100% offline. Free and open source.
meta.twitterTitle: Kodo Forge — Learn to Code in Your Terminal
meta.twitterDescription: A free, open-source TUI platform for mastering web development. 144+ lessons, spaced repetition, 100% offline.

hero.description: Kodo Forge is a free, open-source learning platform that runs entirely in your command line. 144+ interactive lessons, spaced repetition, gamified progress — without browser, without internet, without distractions.

whatIs.p1: Most coding courses run in a browser — surrounded by notifications, social media, and infinite tabs.
whatIs.p1Bold: Kodo Forge takes a radically different approach.
whatIs.p2: It runs as a native application inside your terminal (command line). The same tool that professional developers use every single day to write and deploy software. No ads, no accounts, no tracking. Just you and the code.
whatIs.p3Bold: distraction-free environments dramatically improve learning retention
whatIs.p3End: . Kodo Forge combines this with science-backed techniques like spaced repetition and active recall to help you truly master — not just consume — TypeScript, React, Angular, and full-stack web development.
whatIs.learnCycleSubtitle: Every lesson follows a proven 5-step methodology:

features.title: Why Developers Choose Kodo Forge
features.subtitle: Built from scratch for one purpose: deep-focus, distraction-free learning that actually sticks.

items.title: Instant Startup
items.desc: Launches in under 200ms. No loading screens, no splash pages. A single executable — just double-click and learn.

items.title: Kinetic Reading Engine
items.desc: A custom text progression engine that reveals content at your pace. No mindless scrolling — every section demands your attention.

items.title: Built-in Spaced Repetition
items.desc: Science-backed flashcard system with smart scheduling. Review concepts right before you forget them, with streak tracking.

items.title: 100% Offline & Private
items.desc: No internet required, no telemetry, no accounts, no data collection. Your learning data stays on your machine. Always.

items.title: Data-Driven Architecture
items.desc: Courses are Markdown folders. Drop in your own content — programming, languages, science — and the engine renders it instantly.

items.title: Gamified Progress Tracking
items.desc: Daily streaks, sparkline activity charts, phase completion, and course recommendations. Stay motivated without social media dopamine.

courses.title: 4 Professional Courses — All Free
courses.subtitle: A complete web development learning path. Start with TypeScript fundamentals, then choose Angular, React, or go full-stack with Next.js.
courses.viewAll: View Full Curriculum for All Courses →

howItWorks.title: Start Learning in 3 Steps
howItWorks.subtitle: No sign-up. No credit card. No complicated setup. From download to learning in under 60 seconds.

steps.title: Download
steps.desc: Grab the single executable for Windows, macOS, or Linux. No Node.js, no Python, no admin rights needed. Just one file.

steps.title: Launch
steps.desc: Double-click the file or run it from your terminal. The TUI boots in under 200ms and automatically detects your courses.

steps.title: Learn & Master
steps.desc: Navigate lessons with your keyboard. Read theory, run examples, solve exercises, take quizzes. Your progress is saved automatically.

items.a: Kodo Forge is a free, open-source Terminal User Interface (TUI) learning platform. It lets you learn TypeScript, Angular, React and Next.js directly inside your command line terminal — with zero internet connection, zero dependencies, and zero distractions.
Yes, 100% free and open source under the MIT license. There are no hidden costs, no subscriptions, and no premium tiers. All 144+ lessons across 4 courses are included.

items.q: Do I need an internet connection?
items.a: No. Kodo Forge runs entirely offline. Once you download the executable, everything works without any internet connection. Your progress is saved locally on your machine.

items.q: What programming languages and frameworks can I learn?
items.a: Kodo Forge currently offers courses in TypeScript (44 lessons), Angular (40 lessons), React with TypeScript (40 lessons), and Next.js (20 lessons). The TypeScript course is the foundation — start there.

items.q: How is this different from Udemy, Codecademy, or freeCodeCamp?
items.a: Unlike browser-based platforms, Kodo Forge runs in your terminal — the same tool professional developers use every day. There are no ads, no notifications, no social features. It's designed for deep focus and uses science-backed techniques like spaced repetition and kinetic reading to maximize retention.

items.q: Can I create my own courses?
items.a: Absolutely! Kodo Forge is a data-driven engine. Courses are just folders of Markdown files. You can create courses on any topic — not just programming. Check the documentation for the content authoring guide.
Kodo Forge provides standalone executables for Windows (x64), macOS (Apple Silicon), and Linux (x64). No installation required — just download and run.

cta.description: Join developers who swapped endless browser tabs for deep-focus terminal learning. Download Kodo Forge, open your command line, and start mastering web development today. Free forever.

footer.brandDescription: A zero-dependency terminal learning environment built for deep-focus mastery.

docsPage.title: Docu
docsPage.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Kodo Forge — from getting started to creating your own terminal courses.
docsPage.openSourceDesc: Built by developers, for developers. Contribute, fork, or just study the code. The entire engine is written in clean, well-documented TypeScript.
docsPage.metaDescription: Learn how Kodo Forge works, how to install it, how to create your own terminal courses, and explore the architecture behind the zero-dependency TUI learning engine.

createPage.title: Author Your Own
createPage.subtitle: Kodo Forge isn't just a learning platform — it's an open-source, data-driven reading engine. You can create stunning interactive terminal courses using nothing but simple Markdown files.
createPage.howItWorksP1: The Kodo Forge engine knows absolutely nothing about TypeScript or React. It purely reads a platform.json file and parses the folders you define inside it.
createPage.howItWorksP2: A course is simply a folder on your computer. Inside that folder, you create numbered subfolders for your lessons (e.g. 01-foundations/). Inside those are your Markdown files.
createPage.howItWorksP3: When you launch Kodo Forge, the engine compiles your Markdown into a highly optimized terminal experience — complete with syntax highlighting, annotated code, adaptive depth modes, and auto-generated quizzes.
createPage.writeMarkdownDesc: No proprietary builders. Just write standard Github Flavored Markdown. We support code fences, bold, italic, tables, and blockquotes.
createPage.terminalNativeDesc: Kodo Forge automatically parses your files into a beautiful TUI context, turning blockquotes into Callout Boxes and headings into semantic dividers.
createPage.annotatedCodeDesc: Add `[annotated]` to your code fences, and the engine splits the view to show your inline `// comments` as beautiful side-by-side explanations.
createPage.aiDesc: We've developed a deterministic AI Workflow instruction set to help you rapidly prototype courses. Give the prompt to AI tools like Claude, Gemini, or ChatGPT, and they will fully generate Kodo Forge compatible directory structures and Markdown files for any topic you want to teach!
createPage.metaDescription: Learn how to author your own terminal courses with Kodo Forge. Write Markdown, define a folder structure, and the engine renders a beautiful TUI learning experience.

typescript.tagline: From basics to type-level programming — 44 lessons, 5 phases
typescript.description: The most comprehensive TypeScript course you'll find in a terminal. 44 lessons, 5 phases, from primitives to the Compiler API.
typescript.longDescription:
This isn't another TypeScript tutorial that stops at interfaces and generics. It's a 44-lesson, 82-hour deep dive that takes you from your first type annotation to writing your own type-level programs and manipulating the Compiler AST.

Every lesson follows the proven LEARN cycle: read theory enriched with diagrams and real-world analogies, explore runnable code examples annotated line-by-line, apply your knowledge through progressively harder exercises, reflect via interactive terminal quizzes, and retain everything with compact cheatsheets and spaced repetition.

What sets this apart from every other TypeScript resource: the built-in spaced repetition engine tracks every concept you learn and schedules reviews at scientifically optimal intervals — right before you'd forget. No other course does this. You build genuine, permanent knowledge instead of a fleeting tutorial high.

The curriculum spans everything a professional needs: primitive types, generics, mapped and conditional types, template literals, discriminated unions, branded types, decorators, the full Compiler API, and type-level programming. Phase 5 pushes into expert territory with TypeScript security patterns, fully typed RxJS pipelines, and 15+ GoF/SOLID design patterns implemented type-safely.
typescript.heroQuote: The TypeScript course that remembers what you forget.
targetAudience: JavaScript developers who want to truly understand TypeScript
Self-learners looking for a structured learning path
Developers preparing for Angular, React, or Next.js
Professionals who want to deepen their type system knowledge
whatYouLearn: All primitive types and when to use annotations vs. inference
Generics: from basics to complex higher-order types
Master mapped types, conditional types, and template literal types
Discriminated unions and exhaustive pattern matching
Branded/nominal types for type-safe IDs and opaque types
Result<T,E> patterns for type-safe error handling
The Compiler API and AST manipulation
Type-level programming — computing with the type system
15+ design patterns (GoF, SOLID, Builder, State Machine) implemented type-safely
TypeScript security: recognizing and avoiding dangerous patterns

didacticHighlights.title: Spaced Repetition
didacticHighlights.description: Every concept is automatically added to your review system. The algorithm schedules reviews optimally — right before you'd forget.

didacticHighlights.title: Adaptive Reading Depth
didacticHighlights.description: Every section offers 3 modes: summary, standard, and deep-dive. You decide how deep you want to go.

didacticHighlights.title: Interactive Quizzes
didacticHighlights.description: At the end of every lesson: multiple choice, code gaps, and concept questions — right in the terminal.

didacticHighlights.title: Annotated Code Blocks
didacticHighlights.description: Code examples with line-by-line annotations that explain what each line does and why.

didacticHighlights.title: Review Challenges
didacticHighlights.description: Every 10 lessons: a phase review that combines everything you've learned and tests your understanding.

didacticHighlights.title: Mermaid Diagrams
didacticHighlights.description: Complex relationships are displayed as visual diagrams right in the terminal.

angular.description: Learn Angular from the ground up — with Signals, modern component design, Dependency Injection, and testing. Requires TypeScript Phase 2 and takes you to production level.

react.description: React, done right. With TypeScript from the start. Hook types, component patterns, context, performance optimization, and testing.

nextjs.description: The full-stack framework for React. Learn App Router, Server Components, caching strategies, and production deployment.
